description: Fix anchor scrolling so that heading targets are not hidden behind the sticky TopNav header when clicking table-of-contents links.
qualityChecks: cd docs && npm run build && npm run type-check && npm run lint && npm run knip && npm run format:check

userStories.title: Add scroll-margin-top to heading elements so anchors clear the sticky header
userStories.description: As a docs reader, I want clicking a table-of-contents anchor link to scroll the heading into view below the sticky navigation bar, so that the heading is not hidden behind the header.
acceptanceCriteria: All heading elements (h1 through h6) rendered by the MDX component overrides in docs/components/MDX.tsx include scroll-mt-20 (5rem) in their className
When navigating to a #heading-id URL (either by clicking a TOC link or loading the page with a hash), the heading is visible below the sticky TopNav (which is h-16 / 4rem + 2px border)
The 5rem scroll-margin-top provides comfortable breathing room (matching the TOC's sticky top-20 offset)
No other layout or visual changes — headings retain their existing margin, padding, font, and border styles
cd docs && npm run build passes
cd docs && npm run type-check passes
cd docs && npm run lint passes
cd docs && npm run knip passes
cd docs && npm run format:check passes
userStories.notes:
The fix is in docs/components/MDX.tsx. Add scroll-mt-20 to each heading component's className string.

The heading components are defined at lines 30-47:

  h1: (props) => <Text as="h1" className="mt-10 mb-4 scroll-mt-20 [&:first-child]:mt-0" {...props} />,
  h2: (props) => <Text as="h2" className="mt-10 mb-6 scroll-mt-20 border-b pb-1 [&:first-child]:mt-0" {...props} />,
  h3: (props) => <Text as="h3" className="mt-8 mb-3 scroll-mt-20 [&:first-child]:mt-0" {...props} />,
  h4: (props) => <Text as="h4" className="mt-6 mb-2 scroll-mt-20 [&:first-child]:mt-0" {...props} />,
  h5: (props) => <Text as="h5" className="mt-4 mb-1 scroll-mt-20 [&:first-child]:mt-0" {...props} />,
  h6: (props) => <Text as="h6" className="mt-4 mb-1 scroll-mt-20 [&:first-child]:mt-0" {...props} />,

The TopNav is sticky top-0 with h-16 (4rem) plus a border-b-2 (2px). scroll-mt-20 is 5rem, which clears the header with ~1rem of breathing room. This matches the TOC sidebar's sticky top-20 offset.

Alternative approach (global CSS) was considered but the component-level approach is preferred because it only affects MDX-rendered headings and is explicit about which elements get the scroll offset.
